message:
fix(channel,link,session): harden broadcast permit liveness, self-link policy coherence, and transport error discrimination

Three related fixes that close subtle correctness gaps in the channel,
link, and session-type layers:

broadcast::SendPermit::send — atomically snapshot receiver liveness:
- After committing the message into the ring buffer, the permit now
  snapshots receiver_count with Acquire ordering *before* releasing the
  channel lock. If the count is zero (all receivers dropped between
  reservation and commit), the message is rolled back and send() returns
  0 without advancing total_sent. This prevents a committed-but-
  undeliverable message from occupying a buffer slot and evicting a
  reachable older message on the next send.
- The return value is now the snapshot taken at commit time rather than
  a re-read after unlocking, eliminating a TOCTOU window where a
  late-subscribing receiver could be counted for a message that was
  committed before it existed.
- New test (send_count_excludes_receivers_that_subscribe_after_commit)
  uses a DropBlocker mechanism to deterministically interleave a
  subscribe between the eviction drop and the commit, verifying the
  snapshot semantics.

link::LinkSet — self-link exit policy coherence:
- establish_with_policy() now normalizes both policy slots to policy_a
  when task_a == task_b, ensuring that a self-link has one canonical
  exit policy across introspection (exit_policy_for), mutation
  (set_exit_policy), and delivery (resolve_exits). Previously, the two
  slots could diverge: policy_a might be Trap while policy_b was Ignore,
  causing resolve_exits to consult the wrong slot depending on internal
  ordering.
- set_exit_policy() now updates both slots when it detects a self-link,
  maintaining the invariant after establishment.
- Two new tests verify self-link establishment + resolution and
  post-establishment policy mutation.

obligation::session_types — transport error discrimination:
- Extract map_transport_send_error() and map_transport_recv_error()
  helpers that translate mpsc::SendError and mpsc::RecvError variants
  into the appropriate SessionError:
  - Disconnected → SessionError::Closed
  - Cancelled → SessionError::Cancelled
  - Full/Empty → debug_assert + Closed (should never happen on
    transport-backed sessions with bounded channels)
- Replace all five send/recv/select/offer async methods' opaque
  is_ok()/is_err() checks with explicit match arms through these
  helpers. Previously, a cancelled Cx would surface as
  SessionError::Closed (indistinguishable from a legitimate peer
  disconnect), making it impossible for callers to implement
  cancellation-aware retry or cleanup logic.
- send_async now uses tx.send() directly instead of reserve().map(),
  simplifying the control flow and making the error path explicit.
- Seven new tests cover: cancellation on send, recv, select_left,
  select_right, and offer; plus peer-drop Closed on recv, select,
  and offer—ensuring all transport-backed session transitions correctly
  discriminate between cancellation and closure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
